summary: Focused verification of the two bugs from this sprint. BUG 1 (scroll-to-top on route change) and BUG 2 (SLA=99.5% everywhere) are BOTH fixed. All 3 backend pytest cases pass and all Playwright UI assertions pass across landing, 3 legal pages, portal login, client dashboard, and a back-nav round-trip.
critical_code_review_comments: ScrollToTop.jsx correctly gated by useLocation pathname+hash and mounted inside <BrowserRouter> — good placement.
Seed drift-correction pass in _seed_products() is idempotent and covers all 6 stale SLA variants (dots and commas) — no duplicate migration needed.
test_credentials: Admin admin@intercloud-digital.com / AdminIntercloud2026!  ·  Client demo@client.com / ClientDemo2026!
context_for_next_testing_agent: BUG 1 & BUG 2 verified via /app/backend/tests/test_sla_products.py (3 tests) and a Playwright grep of body.innerText across landing, /legal/terms, /legal/aup, /legal/sla, /portal/login, /portal/client/dashboard, /portal/register — none contained 99.9% / 99.95% / 99.99% / 99,9% / 99,95% / 99,99%. Scroll assertion: scrolled landing to bottom (scrollY=2880), clicked each footer legal link, verified scrollY=0 on destination. Back-nav (landing → /legal/terms → back → /legal/aup) also lands at 0. Portal client dashboard shows '99.5% uptime' string. Note: portal dashboard doesn't have enough content to scroll for the /dashboard→/invoices route-reset test — scrollY stayed at 0 both before and after nav (not a defect, dashboard just fits above the fold).
